free_text: Excitation functions of 3He-particle induced
nuclear reactions on natural nickel with special
reference to the monitoring of beam energy and
intensity
metallic foils of high purity nat-Ni
thickneses of 10.2 mg/cm**2, 8.91 mg/cm**2, and
20 mg/cm**2
Cr/Ni alloy foils thickneses of 20 mg/cm**2
covered energy range from 2
to 36 MeV degradation oh He3 energy was calculated
according to C.F. Williamson et Al., CEA-R342(1966).
the energy of the incident beam was measured by
magnetic deflection and by measuring the separation
time between neighbouring beam bunches. Copper foils
of 7.38 mg/cm**2 and 8.56 mg/cm**2 thicknesses and Ti
foils of 5.97 Mg.Cm**2 and 9.7 mg/cm**2 thicknesses
were inserted into the stack for comparison
the decay data were taken from E.Browne and
R.B.Fierstone, Table of radioactive isotope,Willey,
New York,1986
The incident He3 energy was measured by an
analysing magnet and by measuring the distance of two
neigbouring bunches of particles in the beam
(Kormany, 1994) with an accuracy of 0.2 MeV.
Uncertainty of the energy of the He3 beam along the
stack includes errors in the thicknesses of target
foils, the energy straggling, and the uncertainty of
the energy of the incident beam.
Quadrature sum of individual relative
errors.
detector efficiency       (5%)
number of target nuclei   (2%)
decay data                (3%)
beam current            (5-8%)
peak area determination
and counting statistics (1-6%)
Beam energy determination
BIB section upgrated. Ref.in MONIT-REF
corrected
TS; Major correction in SUBENT 8
including the contribution of the direct nuclear
reaction leading the ground state and the decay of the
isomeric state of 25-Mn-52 and the decay of from
ground and metastable states of 26-Fe-52.
TS;CUM was removed from REACTION line
